mechanism_narrative: A very low FEDFUNDS rate anchors short rates and, when it persists, suppresses term-premium pressure, so the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 20 trading days as markets continue to price an extended easing stance.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains near the lower bound but the 10-year yield rises over the next 20 trading days in most instances, or if the sign accuracy falls below 55% in rolling out-of-sample tests, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a short window, labor-market deterioration signals weaker growth and lower future inflation, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 10-year yield changes are positive in more than 60% of triggered episodes, or if the triggered set shows no yield compression relative to untriggered months at conventional significance levels.

mechanism_narrative: If RSAFS accelerates while HOUST remains weak, the market reads demand as resilient but housing-led disinflation as intact, so the 10-year Treasury yield rises only modestly as growth optimism dominates the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS-HOUST signal is not directionally positive in out-of-sample tests, or if RSAFS changes alone fully explain the effect without the interaction term.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rebound in INDPRO after prior weakness indicates stronger capacity utilization and reduced slack in goods production, which lifts nominal rates and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises by at least 0.4% month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO shocks are followed by lower 10-year yields more often than higher yields, or if the effect disappears after controlling for recent CPIAUCSL changes.

mechanism_narrative: A broad improvement in PAYEMS together with stable or rising retail sales indicates stronger household income and spending, raising growth expectations and moving the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS-RSAFS strength does not outperform single-series models, or if the sign of the 20-day yield response is negative in a majority of expansion episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is accelerating while FEDFUNDS stays unchanged, real policy becomes more accommodative, inflation expectations drift higher, and the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 1.0pp and FEDFUNDS is unchanged over the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration triggers are not followed by higher yields at least 55% of the time, or if the response is indistinguishable from zero after excluding policy-change weeks.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary labor regime, rising PAYEMS after a prior UNRATE spike signals stabilization rather than overheating, so the 10-year Treasury yield falls as markets price slower policy normalization over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE has risen at least 0.5pp over 6 months
failure_condition: Reject if post-spike labor stabilization is followed by persistent yield increases, or if the 60-day forward yield response is not negative in a statistically meaningful fraction of recession-risk episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST after prior GDP weakness indicates demand destruction and weaker construction activity, which lowers inflation pressure and pulls the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth is below 1.5% annualized over the latest available quarter and HOUST falls more than 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if housing weakness during low-GDP-growth periods does not precede lower yields, or if the effect reverses when GDP growth is already strong.
